steps.title: Identify the universal fact
steps.content: The passage explains a characteristic of helium that is always true—not something that happened or is happening at a specific moment.

steps.title: Apply simple present tense to express the universal fact
steps.content:
Simple present tense expresses facts that are always true. “Has” is correct—it states a characteristic true of all helium atoms.

The other choices suggest either past time (had and has had) or ongoing action (is having), which don't fit a timeless characteristic.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
